mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when the trigger activates, or signal loses predictive power during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series diverges, or Rank IC drops below 0.02 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An upward revision in the index of industrial production reflects accelerating manufacturing and utility output, which reinforces short-term market momentum and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal reverses direction during supply-constrained inflationary periods, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with restrictive policy rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity multiples, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses explanatory power when inflation expectations remain anchored, or forward returns turn positive in >50% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic capacity and consumer spending power, supporting corporate earnings trajectories and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or payroll growth fails to correlate with equity returns during structural labor market shifts.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real gross domestic product below the 1.0% threshold signals economic contraction, prompting institutional deleveraging and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold fails to predict negative returns when GDP declines are driven by temporary statistical revisions rather than fundamental demand shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and total employment confirm synchronized supply-side expansion, boosting short-term market sentiment and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal becomes statistically insignificant when supply chain bottlenecks decouple production from employment, or 5-day returns show negative alpha in >55% of cases.
